description_long: This bungalow features a steeply pitched gable roof clad in reddish-brown tiles, with a single skylight on one side. The exterior walls are finished in horizontal beige siding, complemented by a stone foundation base. The front facade includes a small covered porch with wooden steps and railings, and a large window adjacent to the main entrance. Windows are symmetrically placed on the side and rear elevations, with a mix of single and double-pane designs. The structure sits on a neatly trimmed grassy plot, and the overall form is compact and rectangular. The architectural style is simple and functional, with minimal ornamentation.
description: A compact bungalow with a steep gable roof, beige siding, stone foundation, and a small covered front porch. Symmetrical windows and a skylight add to its simple, functional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch, large window, and roof skylight.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting two symmetrically placed windows and the depth of the porch.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by the expansive gable roof and plain siding, with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view featuring a large window and the roof skylight, with the gable end clearly visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped front and side windows
